pages.raw_text:
On a cot in the second ward at
Sacred Heart hospital lies the only
man who escaped with his life after
riding with the runaway train the
entire distance. c. Daniels Is his
name and he claims to be an AL
gerian. He is badly injured, being
bruised and broken in a dozen places.
In broken English he told his story:


II came in from Kalispell last
night. I passed coal to the fireman
on the engine TO pay my passage.
When we got into Hillyard we switch
ed onto a sidetrack where there were
many coal cars. 1 lay down in a lum-
Der car and went to sleep. Pretty
soon awoke and the train was going
at full speed and I thought it had
started into Spokane. wanted to
jump of at first, but the train was
going too fast for me. Soon knew
what was the matter as I could see
no engine. Then the train struck
something and broke in two and 1t
broke several times before it struck
and was wrecked. After that l knew
nothing"


Frank L. Gordon, claim agent for
the Great Northern railway, this
morning stated cause for the break
away Of the cars as follows:
